description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with the default node:20 image and stages build_test, package, and deploy. In the build_test stage add a build-ext-assets job that caches .npm/, runs `npm ci --force`, `./set_version.sh`, `npm run build:ext --progress=false`, `bin/remove_eval.sh`, `npm run ext:build`, moves the generated zip to `web-ext-artifacts/passit.zip` and the `dist` folder to `dist-ext/`, and stores `web-ext-artifacts/` and `dist-ext/` as artifacts; also add a build-web-assets job that caches .npm/, runs `npm ci --force` and `npm run build -- --configuration=production --progress=false`, and archives the `dist/` directory as an artifact; finally add a test job using the node:20-alpine image that caches .npm/, installs Chromium and related tools via `apk add --update --no-cache chromium-chromedriver chromium git python3 make g++`, runs `npm ci --force` and `npm run test -- --progress false --watch=false --browsers=Chromium_CI`. In the package stage add a build-push-docker job that runs only on master or tags, uses the docker:stable image with a docker:dind service, echoes a start message, disables PIPE traps, extracts the version from package.json with `awk '/version/{gsub(/("|,)/,"",$2);print $2}' package.json | head -n 1`, builds the production Dockerfile with `docker build -f Dockerfile.prod -t ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME} -t ${CONTAINER_IMAGE} --build-arg PASSIT_VERSION=$VERSION .`, logs into the GitLab registry, and pushes both tags. In the deploy stage add a manual deploy-chrome-ext job (tags only) using debian:trixie that installs curl, jq, zip, unzip and runs `bin/deploy_chrome.sh`; a manual deploy-firefox-ext job (tags only) that installs `@wext/shipit` via `npm install @wext/shipit` and runs `npm run ext:publish_firefox`; a manual deploy-dockerhub job (tags only) using docker:stable with docker:dind that logs into the GitLab registry, pulls the built image, logs into Docker Hub, tags the image as `${DOCKER_CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}`, and pushes it; and a deploy-staging job (master only) using the alpine/helm image with an empty entrypoint that adds the helm repo and upgrades the release with `helm upgrade passit-staging django-helm-chart/django --set-string image.tag=${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA} --reuse-values`. Include variables CHROME_BIN, CONTAINER_IMAGE, NODE_VERSION, and npm_config_cache as defined.
